meta.setting: Timber wagon bridges over creeks, cuts, and gulches in the northern California mining country
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with crossing trouble, toll pressure, repair work, and armed road control

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Road, mining, logging, and settlement movement through rugged Whiskeytown country.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Wildlife presence along creek corridors and brushy crossings.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Wagon Bridges region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Planks, Toll Chain, and Creek Noise
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: bridge object, water or animal sign, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing bridges as road chokepoints, repair sites, toll disputes, ambush stages, and uneasy meeting ground
closing blockquote that gives a generated bridge sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate bridges as working timber structures that decide who can pass: wagons, riders, pack animals, Native families, cattle, freight, and law papers all bottleneck here.
Give the table reasons to stop: broken planks, rising water, toll claims, sabotaged bolts, a wagon blocking the deck, animal tracks under the bank, or a guard who knows too much.
Use splintered fir, iron spikes, wet rope, mud, creek foam, deer sign, and old repair patches as the language of the page.
A bridge should never be only scenery; it should hold a choice, a witness, or a bill.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the bridge sound like a handshake between two bad roads.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A wagon-bridge scene should ask who owns the crossing today, what broke overnight, and what waits below the deck.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western road and freight language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/wagon-bridges.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make crossings symbolic instead of practical
do not include modern bridge engineering, pavement, guardrails, vehicles, or signage
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about bridges as active road chokepoints and scene engines.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
